generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance CartMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller system for horizontal movement of the lab-scale gantry crane's cart component"
]

instance HoistMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller system for vertical movement of the lab-scale gantry crane's hoist component"
]

instance ElectromagneticPuckPositioner : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electromagnet for precise puck positioning or load handling in the crane's operational envelope"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Rotational position encoder measuring the swing angle of the gantry crane's arm"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleData
]

instance CartPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder tracking the horizontal movement of the gantry crane's cart"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CartPositionData
]

instance HoistPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder tracking the vertical movement of the gantry crane's hoist"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HoistPositionData
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring wind speed for environmental context in the lab environment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData WindSpeedData
]

// C4: Data Transmission Components
instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance CartPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CartPositionEncoder
]

instance HoistPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HoistPositionEncoder
]

instance WindSpeedData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

instance OperationalMetricsData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder, CartPositionEncoder, HoistPositionEncoder
]

// C5: Data Storage Components
instance RealTimePositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time position data stream (position, velocity) from all crane encoders at sub-second intervals"
    DTDFVocab:fromData SwingAngleData, CartPositionData, HoistPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBLoggingService
]

instance HistoricalTrajectoryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical trajectory execution metrics (position, velocity, torque, power consumption) logged at predefined intervals (per second or per cycle)"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalMetricsData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBLoggingService
]

instance EnvironmentalContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Environmental context data including wind speed measurements for operational validation"
    DTDFVocab:fromData WindSpeedData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBLoggingService
]

// C10: Models
instance KinematicTrajectoryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D kinematic model representing the lab-scale gantry crane's structural geometry and motion capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

instance MotionSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model implementing discrete-event or physics-based simulation for crane operations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationService
]

instance CADStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D CAD model (Autodesk Fusion360) representing the physical gantry crane structure and kinematics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance TimescaleDBLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database (TimescaleDB) for storing and querying historical operational data with sub-second granularity"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance MQTTMessageBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol (Eclipse Mosquitto) for low-latency bidirectional communication between physical components and digital twin services"
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalCraneInterface, DataVisualizationService
]

instance PythonControlFramework : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom Python-based framework implementing PID controllers, motion planning, and real-time control algorithms"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

instance GrafanaVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive visualization tool (Grafana) for real-time and historical data visualization alongside digital twin states"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance Fusion360Visualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Autodesk Fusion360 visualization tools for overlaying physical and digital states (trajectory tracking) in 3D/2D"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance CustomPLCInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom-built PLC-like module interfacing with the crane's controller for deterministic timing in control loops"
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating kinematic trajectories based on the crane's structural model for precise motion planning"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation service modeling crane operations for validation and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:provides ManualAdjustmentAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing 3D/2D visualizations of physical and digital states for validation and debugging"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateVisualizationInsight
]

instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Implements closed-loop control algorithms to minimize residual motion errors by dynamically adjusting actuator commands based on real-time kinematic feedback"
    DTDFVocab:provides TrajectoryOptimizationInsight, ManualAdjustmentAction
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Records operational data (position, velocity, torque, power consumption) at predefined intervals into TimescaleDB for offline analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalTrendAnalysisInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Provides interactive 3D/2D visualizations of physical crane state (joint angles, path trajectories) and digital twin model parameters in real-time"
    DTDFVocab:provides StateVisualizationInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Compares real-time data from the physical crane with the digital twin's kinematic model to detect discrepancies and notify operators of validation thresholds"
    DTDFVocab:provides DiscrepancyAlertAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TrajectoryOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time performance optimization insights enabling precise trajectory tracking and adaptive control strategies"
]

instance ManualAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual intervention action triggered when validation thresholds exceed predefined limits, requiring operator attention"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HistoricalTrendAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Long-term trend analysis of operational metrics (position, velocity, torque) for performance validation and maintenance planning"
]

instance StateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Synchronized visualization of physical and digital states enabling continuous validation and debugging of the digital twin model"
]

instance DiscrepancyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Alert notification sent to operators when real-time data validation thresholds are exceeded, prompting manual intervention rather than automatic model updates"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C12: Physical Crane Interface
instance PhysicalCraneInterface : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Python program container interfacing with the crane's controllers to collect real-time state data and transmit to digital twin services"
    DTDFVocab:isContainedIn PythonControlFramework
]

instance PhysicalCraneInterfaceContainer : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Python program container interfacing with the crane's controllers to collect real-time state data and transmit to digital twin services"
]

// C11: Enablers (additional instances)
instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service generating kinematic trajectories from the CAD model for motion planning"
    DTDFVocab:enables KinematicTrajectoryModel
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service implementing physics-based simulation for crane operations"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionSimulationModel
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Service providing CAD-based visualization of the crane structure"
    DTDFVocab:enables CADStructuralModel
]

// C13: Environment
instance LabEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains SwingAngleEncoder, CartPositionEncoder, HoistPositionEncoder, Anemometer,
          CartMotorController, HoistMotorController, ElectromagneticPuckPositioner,
          PhysicalCraneInterface
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains KinematicTrajectoryModel, MotionSimulationModel, CADStructuralModel,
          TrajectoryGeneratorService, SimulationService, VisualizationService,
          OptimalControlService, HistoricalDataLoggingService, DataVisualizationService,
          ContinuousValidationService, TimescaleDBLoggingService, MQTTMessageBroker,
          PythonControlFramework, GrafanaVisualization, Fusion360Visualization,
          CustomPLCInterface, PhysicalCraneInterface
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
  The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** is implemented via the **Control Application**, which receives trajectory commands from the **Trajectory Generator Service** (a kinematics-based service generating optimal movement paths with zero residual swinging motion). The Control Application translates these commands into control signals sent through the **Physical Crane Interface** to the actual crane hardware.

  Command types include trajectory waypoints and motion parameters derived from the kinematics model. The **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** enables operator interaction, allowing manual overrides or adjustments to trajectories. No explicit message formats (e.g., JSON) or authentication mechanisms are described, but the **MQTT Broker** is mentioned for signaling between services (implying control messages may use it indirectly). Feedback systems are implied by the **Continuous Validation Service**, which monitors discrepancies between simulated and real trajectories, though conflict resolution or command execution confirmation are not detailed.

  The Control Application validates commands before execution, ensuring alignment with the crane’s operational constraints (e.g., kinematic limits). No additional protocols (e.g., HTTP/REST) or detailed feedback loops are specified.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** system employs a **low-cost, pre-recorded data-driven approach** with virtual machines hosting InfluxDB and ActiveMQ, suggesting asynchronous or batch-based synchronization rather than explicit real-time requirements. The use of pre-recorded data implies offline or semi-real-time processing without specified latency or granularity constraints.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar leverages a **Raspberry-Pi with sensor inputs** and AutomationML for model-driven digital twin engineering, but no specific synchronization frequencies (e.g., millisecond-to-second granularity) or latency bounds (e.g., ≤100ms) are documented. The reliance on Microsoft Azure tools for runtime visualization/simulation implies cloud-based processing, but temporal specifics (e.g., polling intervals) are not provided.

For the **lab-scale gantry crane**, no details regarding synchronization frequencies, latency requirements, or temporal granularity are mentioned in the provided documents.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplifies a **self-adaptive digital twin constellation** where both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin exhibit **behavioral adaptation** via **InfluxDB + ActiveMQ** integration. The system lacks a formal orchestration layer but relies on **ad-hoc containerization (VMs)** for deployment flexibility. The **Air Quality Management** employs an **AutomationML-based digital twin**, integrated with **Microsoft Azure tools** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction. This constellation includes:
- **Component Integration**: Raspberry Pi (edge) ↔ AutomationML model ↔ Azure cloud services (for prediction).
- **Resource Management**: Azure likely handles scalability for prediction workloads, while AutomationML ensures interoperability.
- **Coordination**: Real-time data streams (via sensors) are processed in parallel for visualization/simulation, with no explicit coordination pattern described.

The **lab-scale gantry crane** uses the **C4 Model** for architecture visualization but does not detail orchestration or coordination mechanisms. The **discussion** highlights that the **ARCHES-PiCar-X** uses **containerization** for modularity but lacks a commercial framework, implying **ad-hoc orchestration**.

**Not in Document** for explicit orchestration patterns or coordination mechanisms in the lab-scale gantry crane.
"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  **Development Stages:**
  - **GreenhouseDT:** Focuses on self-adaptive digital twins for agricultural systems, emphasizing real-time behavioral adaptation of both the physical greenhouse and its digital twin during operation.
  - **Air Quality Management:** Centers on model-driven digital twins for construction engineering, particularly for indoor air quality management and virus prevention. Applications include runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction, suggesting iterative development phases tied to operational needs.

  **Representation Types:**
  - **GreenhouseDT:** Uses pre-recorded data in virtual machines and relies on databases (InfluxDB) and message brokers (ActiveMQ) for real-time data synchronization.
  - **Air Quality Management:** Leverages AutomationML for digital twin representation and Microsoft Azure tools for pipeline implementation, indicating structured data modeling for interoperability.

  **Transition Management:**
  - Both exemplars appear to adopt an **ad-hoc approach** without explicit lifecycle governance frameworks. The transition between physical and digital states is managed via real-time sensor data (Raspberry Pi) and containerized environments (e.g., virtual machines, Docker-like setups).

  **Lifecycle Governance:**
  - **Not explicitly detailed** in the documents. The examples emphasize modular, domain-specific adaptations rather than standardized lifecycle governance.
  "
]

    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** and **Air Quality Management** exemplars do not detail explicit validation methods beyond behavioral adaptation or model-driven engineering frameworks.

    base:desc "
  The **digital twin’s technical connection** relies on a **containerized network infrastructure** deployed on a single personal computer. The **MQTT Broker** serves as the primary communication protocol for inter-service signaling between the **Trajectory Generator Service**, **Simulation Service**, **Continuous Validation Service**, and **Dashboard**. The topology is centralized, with all services hosted locally on the same machine.

  No explicit bandwidth or latency requirements are mentioned, but the system’s real-time dependencies imply minimal latency constraints (e.g., sub-second response times for trajectory validation). Reliability is ensured by the containerized deployment (e.g., Docker) for the **Database**, **MQTT Broker**, and **Dashboard**, though redundancy mechanisms are not detailed. Security protocols are not explicitly described, but the system’s local deployment implies minimal exposure to external threats.

  The **TimescaleDB database** acts as a centralized store for all logged data, with no mention of distributed or sharded architectures. The network topology is implicit: all services communicate via the MQTT broker, with no additional protocols (e.g., HTTP/REST) or peer-to-peer architectures.
  "
]

  Computational requirements are not specified, but the system’s real-time operations imply moderate CPU and memory usage. ]
